pending.body:
finding_summary: Compatibility validation now rejects any bead priority outside 1..=3, even when callers explicitly use legacy-compatible `validate()`. That is a behavioral regression for previously persisted milestone bundles: old plans that used `priority: 0`/`P0` or backlog priorities will now fail handoff/materialization, even though this change was supposed to be additive and compatibility mode exists precisely to keep legacy bundles loadable.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:173-181
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs:2358-2385
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1360-1364
remediation_hint: Apply the `1..=3` restriction only in `BundleValidationMode::Generated`, or widen compatibility mode to accept the historical priority range so legacy bundles still validate while fresh planner output remains constrained.

finding_summary: The new strict planner contract still allows the model to omit `bead_id`, then silently substitutes positional `bead-N` identifiers during validation/canonicalization. Because `RequirementsContract::milestone_bundle()` treats `validate_generated()` as authoritative, planner output can pass without the stable IDs the prompt now requires, leaving dependency and coverage references tied to bead ordering instead of durable identifiers.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:192-236
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1179-1203
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-376
remediation_hint: In generated mode, require `bead_id` to be present and non-empty for every bead, and add a contract test that proves `bead_id: null` is rejected.

finding_summary: `validate_generated()` never requires a generated bead to map to at least one acceptance criterion; it only validates entries if the list is present. That means the strict contract still accepts untraceable beads that contribute to no milestone acceptance criterion, even though this bead added bidirectional bead↔AC mapping as a core requirement.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs
file_refs:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:240-286
- src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs:372-376
remediation_hint: In generated mode, reject empty `bead.acceptance_criteria` lists unless the schema gains an explicit way to represent intentionally non-AC work; add a contract regression test for that omission.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
